name: Amazon Selling Partner
description: Amazon Selling Partner API (SP-API) — REST-based API for programmatic access to Amazon seller data (listings, orders, payments, reports, etc.).

auth_methods.note: SP-API uses Login with Amazon (LWA) OAuth 2.0 to obtain access tokens and requires all SP-API requests to be signed with AWS Signature Version 4 (SigV4) using AWS IAM credentials; 'other' represents the SigV4 request-signing requirement.

credential_access.note: Documentation describes self-registration in the Developer Console to create a security profile and obtain LWA client credentials and instructs developers to create AWS IAM credentials. The docs describe a self-serve onboarding flow but do not explicitly state pricing or paid approval requirements on the public pages fetched; Seller Central pages that might mention fees require sign-in and were not fetchable.

api_surface.note: The SP-API is described as a modern REST-based API covering listings, orders, payments, reports, and more (multiple product areas).

buildability.note: Public documentation provides REST API references and step-by-step self-registration (create security profile, obtain LWA credentials, create AWS IAM credentials). Access to seller-specific data requires that the selling partner authorize the application via the LWA OAuth workflow.

queries: Selling Partner API auth methods Login with Amazon AWS SigV4
registering your application SP-API create security profile LWA client id
SP-API onboarding seller authorization Developer Console
Seller Central app fees appstore fees (sellercentral) - attempted, requires sign-in
